mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings prospects, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 5.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, which amplifies revenue growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during monetary tightening phases, producing negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of rising INDPRO observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlate with rising household income and consumer expenditure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 52% of payroll-growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL breaches the 235.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, which sustains equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive power when GDP growth decouples from retail sales, yielding a negative interaction coefficient in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp increases in the federal funds rate during late-cycle expansions trigger immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate negative returns when rate hikes are fully priced in, resulting in positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of hike months.
